Title: Engineering secondary cell wall deposition in plants

Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pIRX8 (secondary cell wall promoter; inducible/native tissue-specific)
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Structural cell wall component contributing to biomass and saccharification substrate
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: IRX8 promoter served as an NST1-inducible promoter to drive the APFL, enabling enhanced secondary wall deposition in fibres
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter engineering in pIRX8::NST1 construct; downstream phenotyping of wall deposition
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Secondary cell wall-associated expression; induced by NST1
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Promoter active in secondary wall-forming stem tissues
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful secondary-wall-specific promoter for synthetic circuits enhancing biomass deposition
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as promoter element rather than manipulated coding sequence in the core experiment
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IRX8 is a downstream target of NST1. 2. Its promoter was used to express an extra NST1 copy and create an artificial positive feedback loop. 3. This circuit contributed to enhanced fibre secondary wall deposition.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports vessel secondary wall integrity and transport function indirectly through promoter-restricted engineering
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: VND6 promoter enabled vessel-specific restoration of lignin biosynthesis through pVND6::C4H, preserving vessel integrity while reducing fibre lignification
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter validation by pVND6::CADd complementation and pVND6::F5H1 expression in mutant backgrounds; subsequent use in pVND6::C4H constructs
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable vessel-specific promoter for rewiring lignin biosynthesis in crops and trees
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In this paper VND6 functioned mainly as promoter source rather than the manipulated coding sequence of the principal experiment
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. pVND6 was validated as largely vessel-specific. 2. It was used to drive C4H expression and restrict lignin biosynthesis to vessels. 3. This promoter decoupled lignin production from the fibre NST1 network.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pVND6 (vessel-specific, inducible/native tissue-specific) replacing native promoter
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates trans-cinnamic acid at the 4-position to form p-coumaric acid, controlling entry of flux into monolignol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control step for pathway flux; paper states controlling C4H expression should be sufficient to control the entire production of monolignols
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides secondary cell wall reinforcement, vessel integrity, water and nutrient transport support; major determinant of biomass recalcitrance
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Secondary cell walls of vessels and fibres in stems
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor flux directly for monolignol/lignin production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Vessel-specific expression of C4H restores vessel integrity and fertility in c4h mutant while reducing overall stem lignin to about two-thirds of wild type, especially in interfascicular fibres
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant complementation in c4h background with pVND6::C4H; lignin quantification by acetyl bromide; phloroglucinol-HCl staining; growth/fertility assessment; saccharification assays
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Native promoter broader than pVND6; active beyond vasculature as inferred from whole old-leaf anthocyanin accumulation in wild type
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Under pVND6, expression largely restricted to vessel cells
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Stem developmental context; comparison under mild pretreatments for saccharification (hot water, dilute alkali)
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: NST1 regulatory network does not control pVND6-driven C4H expression
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious growth defects after vessel-specific complementation; lignin remains reduced which is beneficial for saccharification
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for lignin rewiring to reduce recalcitrance while preserving vessel function; could be deployed with vessel-specific promoters in crops/trees
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study used the ref3-2 poorly functional c4h allele; homozygous mutant is male sterile, but pVND6::C4H restored fertility and normal growth
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. C4H was selected as the control point to regulate monolignol production because it is an early key step in lignin biosynthesis. 2. Replacing native C4H expression with vessel-specific pVND6 restored vessel integrity and plant growth in c4h mutants. 3. This rewiring reduced lignin deposition in interfascicular fibres and improved saccharification while maintaining healthy plants.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pIRX8 (secondary cell wall promoter; downstream target of NST1; inducible/native tissue-specific)
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Secondary cell wall polysaccharides (cellulose and xylan) with associated lignin modulation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provide mechanical strength and biomass; increase sugar yield after saccharification; reinforce stems
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Stem fibre secondary cell walls, including xylary and interfascicular fibres
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Secondary cell wall biosynthesis and deposition in fibre cells
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Master switch for fibre secondary cell wall deposition; used here to boost wall polysaccharide deposition specifically in stems
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: IRX8 and broader downstream secondary wall gene network involved in cellulose, hemicellulose, and lignin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates downstream secondary cell wall genes including IRX8; positively controls lignin biosynthetic pathway under native network
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation via an artificial positive feedback loop using pIRX8::NST1
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Artificial positive feedback loop (APFL): NST1 induces pIRX8, which drives an extra NST1 copy, further enhancing NST1 expression
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Native secondary cell wall developmental program in stems/fibres
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: pIRX8-driven NST1 overexpression in lignin-rewired plants increases fibre cell wall thickness, cellulose and xylan deposition, biomass density, and saccharification yield without obvious growth defects
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic expression of pIRX8::NST1; semi-quantitative RT-PCR; TEM; confocal Raman microspectroscopy; immunofluorescence with CBM3a and LM10; lignin quantification; saccharification assays
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Native NST1 expressed in stems/fibres involved in secondary wall deposition; semi-quantitative RT-PCR detected native NST1 similarly in wild type and engineered lines
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher total NST1 expression in stems only in lines carrying pIRX8::NST1 APFL
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Stem developmental context; lignin-rewired c4h+pVND6::C4H background
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: IRX8 downstream promoter target within NST1 network
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Constitutive overexpression of master regulators is harmful according to cited background, but APFL design avoided obvious growth defects and over-lignification
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising synthetic regulatory module to enhance cell wall polysaccharide deposition and biomass quality in crops while minimizing pleiotropic effects; may improve lodging resistance and bioenergy traits
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NST1 overexpression caused only slight lignin increase relative to parental rewired line, but lignin stayed below wild type because C4H was disconnected from NST1 control by pVND6
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NST1 was used as the master fibre secondary wall regulator in an artificial positive feedback loop driven by pIRX8. 2. The APFL elevated total NST1 expression in stems and increased fibre wall thickening, cellulose, and xylan deposition. 3. In the lignin-rewired background, NST1 enhancement improved biomass density and saccharification without obvious developmental penalties.
